decisions.reasoning: Post-WWDC sell-off (ATH $317.40 on June 8 to $291 today) appears news-driven rather than fundamental. Q2 services revenue $31B (+16% YoY) intact. EU and China Siri AI blocking is a known, priced-in risk. Valuation stretched at P/E ~38 and forward P/E ~32. Analyst consensus ~$310-320 vs current $291. SMA signal still long but price is below both fast ($302) and slow ($299) SMAs — caution warranted. Not adding given stretched multiple and macro headwinds.

sources.headline: AAPL Stock Slides Following WWDC, But Analysts Broadly Raise Targets
Apple Stock Slips After WWDC 2026: Wall Street Splits Between a $400 Bull Case and a $215 Floor
Apple Intelligence expansion hits roadblocks in China and EU, raising fragmentation concerns

decisions.invalidation: Siri AI EU/China approval or material upside surprise in iPhone supercycle data. Conversely would sell if SMA crosses bearish and P/E re-rates toward 28-30x on missed services growth.

decisions.reasoning: Q3 FY26 fundamentals exceptional: Azure +40%, AI business $37B ARR (+123% YoY), EPS $4.27 vs $4.07 estimate. However stock is -17% YTD vs S&P +7%, SMA signal has flipped to flat (fast SMA $415.99 < slow SMA $417.02). Bear case is real: $190B annualized capex compresses near-term FCF; Google Workspace directly competing with Copilot; enterprise AI adoption lagging projections per bears. No new catalyst today. Holding thesis — Azure cloud dominance + AI monetization — but not adding without technical confirmation.

sources.headline: MSFT stock analysis June 2026 - Azure grew 40%, AI business $37B annual run rate
Microsoft Stock Price Prediction: A New Record High on the Horizon?
Microsoft $190 Billion AI Bet Is Both the Bull Case and Bear Case

decisions.invalidation: Azure growth decelerates below 30% in Q4 FY26, or FCF margin compresses more than 500bp YoY signaling capex is not generating returns. Would also reassess if SMA remains bearish for 5+ sessions.

decisions.reasoning: FY26 revenue $215.94B (+65.47% YoY). Multiple positive catalysts announced today: SharonAI Holdings 6-year compute collaboration, KKR-Nvidia-Vistra $10B+ Helix Digital Infrastructure deal. China Vera CPU strategy targets delivery Aug 2026, recovering export-control-constrained market share. 62 analysts Strong Buy, avg target $298 vs current $205. However SMA signal is flat (fast SMA $211.27 < slow SMA $214.07), and price has pulled back 5.6% from our entry. Not adding without SMA technical confirmation.

sources.headline: SharonAI Holdings announces six-year AI infrastructure collaboration with NVIDIA
Prediction: Nvidia Stock Will Skyrocket in June

decisions.invalidation: US export controls tighten further cutting off China revenue; custom silicon (AWS Trainium, Google TPUs) accelerates market share loss from cloud hyperscalers; data center capex cycle peaks and orders soften.

decisions.reasoning: Only profitable position (+0.78%). Broad market SMA signal long (fast $744.43 > slow $741.01). Acts as macro hedge in portfolio dominated by single-name tech risk. Fed likely on hold through 2026 per CME FedWatch, though 70% odds of 25bp hike by Dec 2026. Keeping position as ballast.

sources.headline: US market drivers in June: Inflation, rates and policy credibility
June 2026 Market Update: Stocks and inflation keep rising

decisions.invalidation: CPI or PCE materially above consensus forcing hawkish Fed pivot; SMA signal turns flat/bearish on SPY itself.

summary: First session — no trades. All three tech positions underwater (AAPL -7%, MSFT -10%, NVDA -6%); MSFT and NVDA SMA signals bearish. Preserving $701 cash pending technical confirmation or new catalysts. Macro: sticky inflation, potential Dec 2026 rate hike, consumer stress.
open_theses: AAPL: post-WWDC sell-the-news dip; watching for EU/China Siri AI resolution or services re-acceleration as next bull catalyst
MSFT: exceptional AI/Azure fundamentals but SMA bearish — will add if fast SMA recrosses above slow SMA with volume
NVDA: FY26 revenue +65%, strong buy consensus, new partnerships; waiting for SMA to turn long before adding
SPY: holding as macro ballast; would trim if SMA signal turns flat
